title: USD Relocates Composition Spec Coverage Ledger

normative_source.rule: Only the AOUSD core specification at aousd/specifications-public v1.0.1 is normative for this ledger. This ledger covers only direct layerRelocates namespace composition.

status_values.implemented: In scope for the relocates-namespace-source contract and covered by goldens.
status_values.partial: A bounded subset is included and later contracts must extend it.
status_values.deferred: Specified behavior intentionally not included in this first relocates contract.
status_values.out_of_scope: Belongs to another subsystem or future skill.

coverage_rules: The provider composes direct layerRelocates and delegates stage traversal to usd-stage-population.
The first provider consumes a base namespace source, not parser-local USDA syntax.
A generated target must not claim automatic ancestral relocate source rewriting or full value resolution from this provider.

obligations: Read source-visible layerRelocates entries as source path to optional target path mappings.
Map source prim and property opinions to the target subtree by path-prefix replacement.
Preserve target-local opinions as stronger than relocated source opinions.
items.deferred_reason: This provider diagnoses invalid ancestral relocate source paths and leaves automatic source-path rewriting out of scope.

obligations: Treat an empty authored target as a delete/removal mapping.
Hide the source prim subtree from composed namespace queries.
Report the entry as deleted in relocates_summary.
Reject pseudo-root and root path relocates.
Reject non-prim source and non-empty target paths.
Reject duplicate source paths and duplicate non-empty target paths.
Reject target-equals-source and target-below-source mappings.
Reject mappings that place a prim at the path of an existing ancestor of the source.
items.deferred_reason: Ancestral relocate source-path normalization is covered by a future contract.

obligations: Diagnose authored local-layer-stack opinions at a relocate source path as recoverable composition errors.
Do not let source-local opinions contribute through the relocated source path.
Retain and apply the otherwise-valid relocate entry instead of ignoring it because source-local opinions were present.
Preserve the ability for non-local source opinions to be relocated when the input source can expose them separately.
Require the consumed reference base source to expose reference-family-only source opinions or equivalent per-family provenance when local and referenced opinions coexist at the relocate source path.
Diagnose a relocate source authored beneath an already-relocated source path as a recoverable composition error.
Ignore the invalid nested relocate entry while continuing to apply other valid relocate entries.
Keep generated implementations total and non-crashing when invalid nested relocate metadata is present.
Apply layer-stack relocate mappings to references authored inside referenced layer stacks.
Apply layer-stack relocate mappings to references authored inside loaded payload layer stacks.
Remap child names, property names, and path-valued fields through the accumulated reference/payload namespace mapping plus the arc-local relocate mapping.
items.deferred_reason: The current implementation covers references and loaded payload nested-reference mappings; inherit and specialize arc-local mappings remain future work.

obligations: Treat target-local opinions as stronger than relocated source opinions in this bounded source.

Place the concrete generated graph so relocates feeds any stronger non-variant providers, including inherits and specializes, before selected variants; variants may consume relocates directly only when no stronger non-variant provider is in scope.
Document that evaluation order is distinct from LIVERPS field-strength order.
Expose relocated composed prims and properties through the namespace-source boundary.
Allow usd-stage-population to populate relocated prims and properties without evaluating relocates itself.
Preserve composed child and property ordering when consumed by stage population.
